texto: Accepting one's age and mortality is a sign that you've now become an adult. Once you realize you no longer fit in the same jeans you did when you were 30, and the spicy foods you loved when you were younger now like to revisit you at 2 AM, you come to realize that with aging comes adjustment. And, aging isn't a bad thing - it sure beats the alternative!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a James Arlen Dennis: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Accepting one's age and mortality is a sign that you've now become an adult. Once you realize you…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, aging, mortality pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mortality.
